utilityName: Palm Beach County Water Utilities Backflow Prevention Program
dueBasis: Palm Beach County Water Utilities uses a Cross-Connection Link and Protect Compliance workflow, requires annual testing after installation, and publishes both utility design standards and a county plumbing contractors list for backflow-related work.
coveredPropertyTypes: County utility customers with protected service connections
coveredDeviceTypes: Backflow prevention assemblies at the service connection

submissionMethods.label: Palm Beach County Water Utilities backflow page
Palm Beach County potable water design standards
Palm Beach County plumbing contractors list

penalties: Palm Beach County Water Utilities makes the county compliance workflow part of service-connection protection. Missing annual testing or using the wrong contractor path can leave the county utility record unresolved.
sourceExcerpt: Palm Beach County is a useful Florida county utility because it pairs a backflow prevention page with county design standards, CLPC compliance workflow language, and a public contractor list.
verdictSummary: Palm Beach County is a strong Florida county utility because it combines county standards, an explicit compliance workflow, and a public contractor list.
whoIsAffected: Commercial, industrial, irrigation, and other Palm Beach County Water Utilities customers whose services require a protected backflow assembly under county standards.
residentialNotes: Palm Beach County is most useful for residential demand where irrigation or another service-connection hazard exists.
The county page is more compliance-workflow driven than consumer-marketing driven.
commercialNotes: Commercial value is good because the county uses a formal compliance workflow and posts contractor and standards links publicly.
The county utility framing makes this a real next-action page rather than a generic Florida overview.

annualTesting.summary: Palm Beach County Water Utilities uses a county compliance workflow and requires annual testing after installation for protected assemblies.
highlights: Annual testing is paired with a county compliance workflow.
County design standards reinforce the service-connection protection rules.
The county publishes a contractor list that supports next-action routing.
workflowSteps: Confirm the service connection is inside Palm Beach County Water Utilities' backflow program.
Complete the annual test after installation or on the recurring county cycle.
Keep the result aligned with the county utility compliance workflow.

irrigation.summary: Palm Beach County's contractor list and service-connection standards make irrigation a real local page, especially in a county where outdoor water use is common.
highlights: Irrigation assemblies still sit inside the same county compliance workflow.
The contractor list gives a clear next action even without a tester-only PDF.
County standards keep the assembly requirement tied to service protection, not just landscaping.
workflowSteps: Confirm the irrigation service needs county protection.
Use a contractor familiar with the county utility workflow.
Keep the annual testing record current.

fireLine.summary: Palm Beach County is less fire-list specific than West Palm Beach, but the county standards still make protected high-hazard services worth a fire-line page.
highlights: County standards govern protected service-connection devices.
The same compliance workflow still applies on protected higher-hazard services.
The contractor list helps route work into a county-friendly path.
workflowSteps: Confirm the service is a higher-hazard or protected service connection.
Use a contractor familiar with county standards.
Keep the annual testing and documentation cycle aligned with the county workflow.
Identify whether Palm Beach County Water Utilities requires a protected assembly on the service.
Complete installation or annual testing on the county schedule.
Use the county contractor list and standards as needed.
Keep the county utility compliance record current.
failureHighlights: Palm Beach County uses a named compliance workflow instead of vague county copy.
County standards and contractor links are both public.
The utility page is usable for irrigation and other service-connection intent.

costBand.testingRange: Testing is market-priced, but county workflow and contractor-routing rules shape the actual path.
costBand.repairRetestRange: Repair and retest pricing varies with assembly size and whether the case is routine irrigation or a higher-hazard protected connection.
costBand.pricingNotes: The local value is in county workflow clarity, not a public county fee schedule.
